title: Java Var Text Block

completionChecks.prompt: What does message contain when name is "Ada"?
choices: Hello, Ada then Keep practicing Java. on the next line
Hello, %s then Keep practicing Java., with %s left unreplaced
Hello, Ada with the leading spaces preserved
Compilation fails because a text block cannot call formatted
completionChecks.explanation: The closing delimiter aligns with the text, so the incidental leading spaces are stripped; formatted replaces %s with "Ada", giving "Hello, Ada" then "Keep practicing Java." on the next line.

completionChecks.prompt: What type does var infer for message?
completionChecks.explanation: A text block is a String literal, and formatted returns another String, so var infers String.

completionChecks.prompt: What happens if the code is changed to var message; and the text block assignment is placed on the next line?
choices: It compiles and var infers String from the later assignment
Compilation fails because a local var declaration requires an initializer
message is inferred as Object until the assignment runs
A NullPointerException is thrown before formatted runs
completionChecks.explanation: Local variable type inference happens at the declaration, so var cannot wait for a later assignment to discover the type.
